{{Quote|Here's your chance to beat up some helpless opponents. It's not that much of a contest.|[[Commander/Legends|Commander]] [[Wex Dafid]] briefs recruits before recreating Dev's mission|<ref name="X-Wing: The Official Strategy Guide">''[[X-Wing: The Official Strategy Guide]]''</ref>}}
'''Dev''' was a [[Starfighter/Legends|starfighter]] [[Rebel pilot/Legends|pilot]] in the [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Alliance to Restore the Republic]] during the [[Galactic Civil War/Legends|Galactic Civil War]]. During an early training [[Raid in the Senex system|mission]] in a [[T-65 X-wing starfighter/Legends|T-65 X-wing starfighter]], Dev accidentally entered [[Hyperspace/Legends|hyperspace]] and emerged in an [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Imperial]] training ground. Mistaking Imperial [[Starship/Legends|craft]] for exercise targets, he wiped them out before his [[R2-series astromech droid/Legends|astromech]] notified him he was in Imperial [[Realspace/Legends|space]], and he returned to the Alliance forces.

==Biography==
Dev joined the [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Alliance to Restore the Republic]] at some point prior to [[Operation Strike Fear]], a major offensive launched against the Alliance by the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]]<ref name="SW:XW" /> in [[1 BBY/Legends|1 BBY]].<ref name="1BBY">''[[X-Wing: The Official Strategy Guide]]'' begins after the [[T-65 X-wing starfighter/Legends|X-wing starfighter]] entered service with the [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Alliance to Restore the Republic]], which ''[[The Essential Guide to Warfare]]'' places in [[1 BBY/Legends|1 BBY]]. ''X-Wing: The Official Strategy Guide'' establishes that the [[Battle of Tatooine (Galactic Civil War)|capture of Leia Organa]] occurs at the end of [[Star Wars: X-Wing (video game)|''Star Wars: X-Wing'']]{{'s}} second tour of duty. Organa's capture takes place on 35:3:3 under the [[Great ReSynchronization]] dating system, placing it at the beginning of [[0 BBY/Legends|0 BBY]] according to [[Template:YearMathGRS|the following explanation]]. Events in ''X-Wing: The Official Strategy Guide'' before the end of the second tour of duty therefore take place in 1 BBY.</ref> Entering the [[Alliance Starfighter Corps/Legends|Alliance Starfighter Corps]], he began training as a [[Starfighter/Legends|starfighter]] [[Rebel pilot/Legends|pilot]]. Before beginning combat training, [[Pilot/Legends|pilots]] were required to display their basic flight skills by completing the [[pilot proving grounds]], commonly referred to as "[[The Maze (pilot proving ground)|The Maze]]," a series of platforms containing gates that needed to be passed through in sequence. On his first run through the course, Dev easily passed the first six gates, but as he banked his [[T-65 X-wing starfighter/Legends|T-65 X-wing starfighter]] toward the next gate, the rookie accidentally made the jump through [[Hyperspace/Legends|hyperspace]] to the [[Senex system/Legends|Senex system]], [[Raid in the Senex system|emerging]] in an Imperial training ground.<ref name="SW:XW" />

[[File:DevSidestep-XW.png|thumb|left|250px|Dev's sidestep was recreated as a training simulation.]]
Thinking that the Imperial ships were part of the course, Dev opened fire on the stationary Imperial [[TIE series/Legends|TIE fighters]] and [[Class-A cargo container]]s, as well as a [[Delta-class DX-9 stormtrooper transport|''Delta''-class DX-9 stormtrooper transport]] and the [[BFF-1 bulk freighter/Legends|BFF-1 bulk freighter]] ''[[Rouh]]''. With his concentration on destroying the enemy targets, it took Dev some time to notice the message from his [[R2-series astromech droid/Legends|R2-series astromech droid]] warning that he was in Imperial space and should return to the proving ground. Though surprised and discomposed by the experience, Dev was able to return safely to the Alliance forces.<ref name="SW:XW" />

The story of how Dev turned his mistake into a success became somewhat famous among Alliance pilots. The event was later recreated as a training [[Flight simulator/Legends|simulation]], with rookie pilots challenged to destroy all craft at the Imperial training ground within fifteen [[Standard minute/Legends|minutes]].<ref name="SW:XW" />

==Personality and traits==
Dev was an able pilot, capable of destroying several Imperial targets before undergoing combat training. However, during his early training, he displayed a lack of familiarity with the systems of his fighter, accidentally entering hyperspace and failing to notice a warning from his [[Astromech droid/Legends|astromech droid]].<ref name="SW:XW" />

==Behind the scenes==
Dev was created for the [[1993]] [[LucasArts]] [[Star Wars video games|video game]] [[Star Wars: X-Wing (video game)|''Star Wars: X-Wing'']]. Although he does not appear in the game itself, he is mentioned in the briefing for the game's first X-wing training mission, "Dev's Sidestep," a recreation of his famous training run.<ref name="SW:XW" />
